messages.content:
You are playing NetHack. Each turn you receive: stats, message, map, inventory.
Reply with ONLY one action word: north south east west northeast southeast southwest northwest wait search eat descend ascend

## Map symbols
@ = you
> = DOWN stairs — stand on it and use "descend" to go deeper. THIS IS YOUR GOAL.
< = UP stairs — do NOT use ascend. < is NOT the exit, it goes the wrong way.
. = floor  # = corridor  + = closed door (walk into it to open)
| - = walls  $ = gold  % = food  f/d = pet
^ = trap (avoid)  _ = altar  { = fountain

## Decision priority each turn

STEP 1: Is > visible on the map? → Move toward it. When standing on it, use "descend".
STEP 2: Is # or + visible? → Move toward it. Corridors and doors lead to new rooms.
STEP 3: None of the above visible? → You are stuck in a room. Use "search" up to 3 times to find hidden doors. Then try moving in a direction you have NOT tried yet.

## Anti-loop rules (CRITICAL)

- If the [LOOP WARNING] tag appears in your input, you are going in circles. IMMEDIATELY pick a completely different direction or use "search".
- Never walk the same rectangular path twice. If you have visited all visible floor tiles, there MUST be a hidden door — use "search".
- Do NOT walk east then west then east then west. That wastes turns.
- If the last 3 actions were the same direction and the map did not change, STOP and try a perpendicular direction.

## Other rules

- READ THE MESSAGE every turn. "can't go" or "It's a wall" = blocked, pick another direction immediately.
- "You swap places with your little dog" = your pet is in the way, move a different direction.
- Never repeat a blocked action.

Reply with just the action word, nothing else.

## Known monsters
- newt (n): threat=low. Can drain strength or dexterity on hit. Do NOT melee as wizard. Let pet handle it.
- jackal (d): threat=low. Fast mover, low damage. Let pet handle or flee if HP < 60%.
- fox (d): threat=low-medium. Faster than jackal. Flee if HP < 60%, especially if combined with other enemies.
- kitten (f): threat=none. Your starting pet. Do NOT attack. Let it fight for you. Use wait to let it move first.
- grid bug (x): threat=very low. Only moves in cardinal directions. Easy to dodge diagonally. Low XP.
- kobold (k): threat=low. Weak melee. Sometimes carries items. Let pet handle.
- goblin (o): threat=low. Weak early enemy. Can carry items. Avoid melee as wizard.

## Known items
- potion of healing [potion]: Restores HP. Blessed: full heal. Cursed: reduces max HP. | Use when HP < 60%. Do NOT use if cursed. Drink with 'drink' action.
- potion of extra healing [potion]: Restores more HP than potion of healing. Also cures blindness. | Save for emergencies (HP < 40%). Higher priority than regular healing.
- potion of full healing [potion]: Fully restores HP and increases max HP. | Most valuable healing item. Only use when near death.
- scroll of identify [scroll]: Identifies one or more items in inventory. | Use to identify unknown potions/scrolls/rings before using them.
- scroll of teleportation [scroll]: Teleports you to a random location on the current level. | Useful escape tool when surrounded. Cursed version confuses you instead.
- food ration [food]: Fills nutrition. Most filling food item. | Eat when Hungry or Weak. Do not wait until Fainting.
- quarterstaff [weapon]: Wizard starting weapon. Two-handed, decent early damage. | Keep equipped as wizard. Do not swap unless you find something clearly better.

Past lessons:
Past lessons:
- Depth 0, Turn 5000: Died on level 1 with 0 HP, likely from an early combat encounter while poorly equipped.
  * Prioritize finding basic armor and a weapon immediately on level 1 to survive initial monsters.
  * Do not explore deeper or engage in combat with only starting robes and no means of defense or escape.
  * Use early turns to search for a dagger in a sink or a weapon shop, and consider eating a corpse to gain intrinsic resistance if safe.

Current goal: Find a door or corridor to exit this room and explore further.

messages.content:
You are playing NetHack. Each turn you receive: stats, message, map, inventory.
Reply with ONLY one action word: north south east west northeast southeast southwest northwest wait search eat descend ascend

## Map symbols
@ = you
> = DOWN stairs — stand on it and use "descend" to go deeper. THIS IS YOUR GOAL.
< = UP stairs — do NOT use ascend. < is NOT the exit, it goes the wrong way.
. = floor  # = corridor  + = closed door (walk into it to open)
| - = walls  $ = gold  % = food  f/d = pet
^ = trap (avoid)  _ = altar  { = fountain

## Decision priority each turn

STEP 1: Is > visible on the map? → Move toward it. When standing on it, use "descend".
STEP 2: Is # or + visible? → Move toward it. Corridors and doors lead to new rooms.
STEP 3: None of the above visible? → You are stuck in a room. Use "search" up to 3 times to find hidden doors. Then try moving in a direction you have NOT tried yet.

## Anti-loop rules (CRITICAL)

- If the [LOOP WARNING] tag appears in your input, you are going in circles. IMMEDIATELY pick a completely different direction or use "search".
- Never walk the same rectangular path twice. If you have visited all visible floor tiles, there MUST be a hidden door — use "search".
- Do NOT walk east then west then east then west. That wastes turns.
- If the last 3 actions were the same direction and the map did not change, STOP and try a perpendicular direction.

## Other rules

- READ THE MESSAGE every turn. "can't go" or "It's a wall" = blocked, pick another direction immediately.
- "You swap places with your little dog" = your pet is in the way, move a different direction.
- Never repeat a blocked action.

Reply with just the action word, nothing else.

## Known monsters
- newt (n): threat=low. Can drain strength or dexterity on hit. Do NOT melee as wizard. Let pet handle it.
- jackal (d): threat=low. Fast mover, low damage. Let pet handle or flee if HP < 60%.
- fox (d): threat=low-medium. Faster than jackal. Flee if HP < 60%, especially if combined with other enemies.
- kitten (f): threat=none. Your starting pet. Do NOT attack. Let it fight for you. Use wait to let it move first.
- grid bug (x): threat=very low. Only moves in cardinal directions. Easy to dodge diagonally. Low XP.
- kobold (k): threat=low. Weak melee. Sometimes carries items. Let pet handle.
- goblin (o): threat=low. Weak early enemy. Can carry items. Avoid melee as wizard.

## Known items
- potion of healing [potion]: Restores HP. Blessed: full heal. Cursed: reduces max HP. | Use when HP < 60%. Do NOT use if cursed. Drink with 'drink' action.
- potion of extra healing [potion]: Restores more HP than potion of healing. Also cures blindness. | Save for emergencies (HP < 40%). Higher priority than regular healing.
- potion of full healing [potion]: Fully restores HP and increases max HP. | Most valuable healing item. Only use when near death.
- scroll of identify [scroll]: Identifies one or more items in inventory. | Use to identify unknown potions/scrolls/rings before using them.
- scroll of teleportation [scroll]: Teleports you to a random location on the current level. | Useful escape tool when surrounded. Cursed version confuses you instead.
- food ration [food]: Fills nutrition. Most filling food item. | Eat when Hungry or Weak. Do not wait until Fainting.
- quarterstaff [weapon]: Wizard starting weapon. Two-handed, decent early damage. | Keep equipped as wizard. Do not swap unless you find something clearly better.

Past lessons:
Past lessons:
- Depth 0, Turn 5000: Died on level 1 with 0 HP, likely from an early combat encounter while poorly equipped.
  * Prioritize finding basic armor and a weapon immediately on level 1 to survive initial monsters.
  * Do not explore deeper or engage in combat with only starting robes and no means of defense or escape.
  * Use early turns to search for a dagger in a sink or a weapon shop, and consider eating a corpse to gain intrinsic resistance if safe.

Current goal: Find a door or corridor to exit this room and explore further.

messages.content:
You are playing NetHack. Each turn you receive: stats, message, map, inventory.
Reply with ONLY one action word: north south east west northeast southeast southwest northwest wait search eat descend ascend

## Map symbols
@ = you
> = DOWN stairs — stand on it and use "descend" to go deeper. THIS IS YOUR GOAL.
< = UP stairs — do NOT use ascend. < is NOT the exit, it goes the wrong way.
. = floor  # = corridor  + = closed door (walk into it to open)
| - = walls  $ = gold  % = food  f/d = pet
^ = trap (avoid)  _ = altar  { = fountain

## Decision priority each turn

STEP 1: Is > visible on the map? → Move toward it. When standing on it, use "descend".
STEP 2: Is # or + visible? → Move toward it. Corridors and doors lead to new rooms.
STEP 3: None of the above visible? → You are stuck in a room. Use "search" up to 3 times to find hidden doors. Then try moving in a direction you have NOT tried yet.

## Anti-loop rules (CRITICAL)

- If the [LOOP WARNING] tag appears in your input, you are going in circles. IMMEDIATELY pick a completely different direction or use "search".
- Never walk the same rectangular path twice. If you have visited all visible floor tiles, there MUST be a hidden door — use "search".
- Do NOT walk east then west then east then west. That wastes turns.
- If the last 3 actions were the same direction and the map did not change, STOP and try a perpendicular direction.

## Other rules

- READ THE MESSAGE every turn. "can't go" or "It's a wall" = blocked, pick another direction immediately.
- "You swap places with your little dog" = your pet is in the way, move a different direction.
- Never repeat a blocked action.

Reply with just the action word, nothing else.

## Known monsters
- newt (n): threat=low. Can drain strength or dexterity on hit. Do NOT melee as wizard. Let pet handle it.
- jackal (d): threat=low. Fast mover, low damage. Let pet handle or flee if HP < 60%.
- fox (d): threat=low-medium. Faster than jackal. Flee if HP < 60%, especially if combined with other enemies.
- kitten (f): threat=none. Your starting pet. Do NOT attack. Let it fight for you. Use wait to let it move first.
- grid bug (x): threat=very low. Only moves in cardinal directions. Easy to dodge diagonally. Low XP.
- kobold (k): threat=low. Weak melee. Sometimes carries items. Let pet handle.
- goblin (o): threat=low. Weak early enemy. Can carry items. Avoid melee as wizard.

## Known items
- potion of healing [potion]: Restores HP. Blessed: full heal. Cursed: reduces max HP. | Use when HP < 60%. Do NOT use if cursed. Drink with 'drink' action.
- potion of extra healing [potion]: Restores more HP than potion of healing. Also cures blindness. | Save for emergencies (HP < 40%). Higher priority than regular healing.
- potion of full healing [potion]: Fully restores HP and increases max HP. | Most valuable healing item. Only use when near death.
- scroll of identify [scroll]: Identifies one or more items in inventory. | Use to identify unknown potions/scrolls/rings before using them.
- scroll of teleportation [scroll]: Teleports you to a random location on the current level. | Useful escape tool when surrounded. Cursed version confuses you instead.
- food ration [food]: Fills nutrition. Most filling food item. | Eat when Hungry or Weak. Do not wait until Fainting.
- quarterstaff [weapon]: Wizard starting weapon. Two-handed, decent early damage. | Keep equipped as wizard. Do not swap unless you find something clearly better.

Past lessons:
Past lessons:
- Depth 0, Turn 5000: Died on level 1 with 0 HP, likely from an early combat encounter while poorly equipped.
  * Prioritize finding basic armor and a weapon immediately on level 1 to survive initial monsters.
  * Do not explore deeper or engage in combat with only starting robes and no means of defense or escape.
  * Use early turns to search for a dagger in a sink or a weapon shop, and consider eating a corpse to gain intrinsic resistance if safe.

Current goal: Find a door or corridor to exit this room and explore further.

messages.content:
You are playing NetHack. Each turn you receive: stats, message, map, inventory.
Reply with ONLY one action word: north south east west northeast southeast southwest northwest wait search eat descend ascend

## Map symbols
@ = you
> = DOWN stairs — stand on it and use "descend" to go deeper. THIS IS YOUR GOAL.
< = UP stairs — do NOT use ascend. < is NOT the exit, it goes the wrong way.
. = floor  # = corridor  + = closed door (walk into it to open)
| - = walls  $ = gold  % = food  f/d = pet
^ = trap (avoid)  _ = altar  { = fountain

## Decision priority each turn

STEP 1: Is > visible on the map? → Move toward it. When standing on it, use "descend".
STEP 2: Is # or + visible? → Move toward it. Corridors and doors lead to new rooms.
STEP 3: None of the above visible? → You are stuck in a room. Use "search" up to 3 times to find hidden doors. Then try moving in a direction you have NOT tried yet.

## Anti-loop rules (CRITICAL)

- If the [LOOP WARNING] tag appears in your input, you are going in circles. IMMEDIATELY pick a completely different direction or use "search".
- Never walk the same rectangular path twice. If you have visited all visible floor tiles, there MUST be a hidden door — use "search".
- Do NOT walk east then west then east then west. That wastes turns.
- If the last 3 actions were the same direction and the map did not change, STOP and try a perpendicular direction.

## Other rules

- READ THE MESSAGE every turn. "can't go" or "It's a wall" = blocked, pick another direction immediately.
- "You swap places with your little dog" = your pet is in the way, move a different direction.
- Never repeat a blocked action.

Reply with just the action word, nothing else.

## Known monsters
- newt (n): threat=low. Can drain strength or dexterity on hit. Do NOT melee as wizard. Let pet handle it.
- jackal (d): threat=low. Fast mover, low damage. Let pet handle or flee if HP < 60%.
- fox (d): threat=low-medium. Faster than jackal. Flee if HP < 60%, especially if combined with other enemies.
- kitten (f): threat=none. Your starting pet. Do NOT attack. Let it fight for you. Use wait to let it move first.
- grid bug (x): threat=very low. Only moves in cardinal directions. Easy to dodge diagonally. Low XP.
- kobold (k): threat=low. Weak melee. Sometimes carries items. Let pet handle.
- goblin (o): threat=low. Weak early enemy. Can carry items. Avoid melee as wizard.

## Known items
- potion of healing [potion]: Restores HP. Blessed: full heal. Cursed: reduces max HP. | Use when HP < 60%. Do NOT use if cursed. Drink with 'drink' action.
- potion of extra healing [potion]: Restores more HP than potion of healing. Also cures blindness. | Save for emergencies (HP < 40%). Higher priority than regular healing.
- potion of full healing [potion]: Fully restores HP and increases max HP. | Most valuable healing item. Only use when near death.
- scroll of identify [scroll]: Identifies one or more items in inventory. | Use to identify unknown potions/scrolls/rings before using them.
- scroll of teleportation [scroll]: Teleports you to a random location on the current level. | Useful escape tool when surrounded. Cursed version confuses you instead.
- food ration [food]: Fills nutrition. Most filling food item. | Eat when Hungry or Weak. Do not wait until Fainting.
- quarterstaff [weapon]: Wizard starting weapon. Two-handed, decent early damage. | Keep equipped as wizard. Do not swap unless you find something clearly better.

Past lessons:
Past lessons:
- Depth 0, Turn 5000: Died on level 1 with 0 HP, likely from an early combat encounter while poorly equipped.
  * Prioritize finding basic armor and a weapon immediately on level 1 to survive initial monsters.
  * Do not explore deeper or engage in combat with only starting robes and no means of defense or escape.
  * Use early turns to search for a dagger in a sink or a weapon shop, and consider eating a corpse to gain intrinsic resistance if safe.

messages.content:
You are playing NetHack. Each turn you receive: stats, message, map, inventory.
Reply with ONLY one action word: north south east west northeast southeast southwest northwest wait search eat descend ascend

## Map symbols
@ = you
> = DOWN stairs — stand on it and use "descend" to go deeper. THIS IS YOUR GOAL.
< = UP stairs — do NOT use ascend. < is NOT the exit, it goes the wrong way.
. = floor  # = corridor  + = closed door (walk into it to open)
| - = walls  $ = gold  % = food  f/d = pet
^ = trap (avoid)  _ = altar  { = fountain

## Decision priority each turn

STEP 1: Is > visible on the map? → Move toward it. When standing on it, use "descend".
STEP 2: Is # or + visible? → Move toward it. Corridors and doors lead to new rooms.
STEP 3: None of the above visible? → You are stuck in a room. Use "search" up to 3 times to find hidden doors. Then try moving in a direction you have NOT tried yet.

## Anti-loop rules (CRITICAL)

- If the [LOOP WARNING] tag appears in your input, you are going in circles. IMMEDIATELY pick a completely different direction or use "search".
- Never walk the same rectangular path twice. If you have visited all visible floor tiles, there MUST be a hidden door — use "search".
- Do NOT walk east then west then east then west. That wastes turns.
- If the last 3 actions were the same direction and the map did not change, STOP and try a perpendicular direction.

## Other rules

- READ THE MESSAGE every turn. "can't go" or "It's a wall" = blocked, pick another direction immediately.
- "You swap places with your little dog" = your pet is in the way, move a different direction.
- Never repeat a blocked action.

Reply with just the action word, nothing else.

## Known monsters
- newt (n): threat=low. Can drain strength or dexterity on hit. Do NOT melee as wizard. Let pet handle it.
- jackal (d): threat=low. Fast mover, low damage. Let pet handle or flee if HP < 60%.
- fox (d): threat=low-medium. Faster than jackal. Flee if HP < 60%, especially if combined with other enemies.
- kitten (f): threat=none. Your starting pet. Do NOT attack. Let it fight for you. Use wait to let it move first.
- grid bug (x): threat=very low. Only moves in cardinal directions. Easy to dodge diagonally. Low XP.
- kobold (k): threat=low. Weak melee. Sometimes carries items. Let pet handle.
- goblin (o): threat=low. Weak early enemy. Can carry items. Avoid melee as wizard.

## Known items
- potion of healing [potion]: Restores HP. Blessed: full heal. Cursed: reduces max HP. | Use when HP < 60%. Do NOT use if cursed. Drink with 'drink' action.
- potion of extra healing [potion]: Restores more HP than potion of healing. Also cures blindness. | Save for emergencies (HP < 40%). Higher priority than regular healing.
- potion of full healing [potion]: Fully restores HP and increases max HP. | Most valuable healing item. Only use when near death.
- scroll of identify [scroll]: Identifies one or more items in inventory. | Use to identify unknown potions/scrolls/rings before using them.
- scroll of teleportation [scroll]: Teleports you to a random location on the current level. | Useful escape tool when surrounded. Cursed version confuses you instead.
- food ration [food]: Fills nutrition. Most filling food item. | Eat when Hungry or Weak. Do not wait until Fainting.
- quarterstaff [weapon]: Wizard starting weapon. Two-handed, decent early damage. | Keep equipped as wizard. Do not swap unless you find something clearly better.

Past lessons:
Past lessons:
- Depth 0, Turn 5000: Died on level 1 with 0 HP, likely from an early combat encounter while poorly equipped.
  * Prioritize finding basic armor and a weapon immediately on level 1 to survive initial monsters.
  * Do not explore deeper or engage in combat with only starting robes and no means of defense or escape.
  * Use early turns to search for a dagger in a sink or a weapon shop, and consider eating a corpse to gain intrinsic resistance if safe.

Current goal: Find a door or corridor to exit this room and explore further.

messages.content:
You are playing NetHack. Each turn you receive: stats, message, map, inventory.
Reply with ONLY one action word: north south east west northeast southeast southwest northwest wait search eat descend ascend

## Map symbols
@ = you
> = DOWN stairs — stand on it and use "descend" to go deeper. THIS IS YOUR GOAL.
< = UP stairs — do NOT use ascend. < is NOT the exit, it goes the wrong way.
. = floor  # = corridor  + = closed door (walk into it to open)
| - = walls  $ = gold  % = food  f/d = pet
^ = trap (avoid)  _ = altar  { = fountain

## Decision priority each turn

STEP 1: Is > visible on the map? → Move toward it. When standing on it, use "descend".
STEP 2: Is # or + visible? → Move toward it. Corridors and doors lead to new rooms.
STEP 3: None of the above visible? → You are stuck in a room. Use "search" up to 3 times to find hidden doors. Then try moving in a direction you have NOT tried yet.

## Anti-loop rules (CRITICAL)

- If the [LOOP WARNING] tag appears in your input, you are going in circles. IMMEDIATELY pick a completely different direction or use "search".
- Never walk the same rectangular path twice. If you have visited all visible floor tiles, there MUST be a hidden door — use "search".
- Do NOT walk east then west then east then west. That wastes turns.
- If the last 3 actions were the same direction and the map did not change, STOP and try a perpendicular direction.

## Other rules

- READ THE MESSAGE every turn. "can't go" or "It's a wall" = blocked, pick another direction immediately.
- "You swap places with your little dog" = your pet is in the way, move a different direction.
- Never repeat a blocked action.

Reply with just the action word, nothing else.

## Known monsters
- newt (n): threat=low. Can drain strength or dexterity on hit. Do NOT melee as wizard. Let pet handle it.
- jackal (d): threat=low. Fast mover, low damage. Let pet handle or flee if HP < 60%.
- fox (d): threat=low-medium. Faster than jackal. Flee if HP < 60%, especially if combined with other enemies.
- kitten (f): threat=none. Your starting pet. Do NOT attack. Let it fight for you. Use wait to let it move first.
- grid bug (x): threat=very low. Only moves in cardinal directions. Easy to dodge diagonally. Low XP.
- kobold (k): threat=low. Weak melee. Sometimes carries items. Let pet handle.
- goblin (o): threat=low. Weak early enemy. Can carry items. Avoid melee as wizard.

## Known items
- potion of healing [potion]: Restores HP. Blessed: full heal. Cursed: reduces max HP. | Use when HP < 60%. Do NOT use if cursed. Drink with 'drink' action.
- potion of extra healing [potion]: Restores more HP than potion of healing. Also cures blindness. | Save for emergencies (HP < 40%). Higher priority than regular healing.
- potion of full healing [potion]: Fully restores HP and increases max HP. | Most valuable healing item. Only use when near death.
- scroll of identify [scroll]: Identifies one or more items in inventory. | Use to identify unknown potions/scrolls/rings before using them.
- scroll of teleportation [scroll]: Teleports you to a random location on the current level. | Useful escape tool when surrounded. Cursed version confuses you instead.
- food ration [food]: Fills nutrition. Most filling food item. | Eat when Hungry or Weak. Do not wait until Fainting.
- quarterstaff [weapon]: Wizard starting weapon. Two-handed, decent early damage. | Keep equipped as wizard. Do not swap unless you find something clearly better.

Past lessons:
Past lessons:
- Depth 0, Turn 5000: Died on level 1 with 0 HP, likely from an early combat encounter while poorly equipped.
  * Prioritize finding basic armor and a weapon immediately on level 1 to survive initial monsters.
  * Do not explore deeper or engage in combat with only starting robes and no means of defense or escape.
  * Use early turns to search for a dagger in a sink or a weapon shop, and consider eating a corpse to gain intrinsic resistance if safe.

Current goal: Find a door or corridor to exit this room and explore further.

messages.content:
You are playing NetHack. Each turn you receive: stats, message, map, inventory.
Reply with ONLY one action word: north south east west northeast southeast southwest northwest wait search eat descend ascend

## Map symbols
@ = you
> = DOWN stairs — stand on it and use "descend" to go deeper. THIS IS YOUR GOAL.
< = UP stairs — do NOT use ascend. < is NOT the exit, it goes the wrong way.
. = floor  # = corridor  + = closed door (walk into it to open)
| - = walls  $ = gold  % = food  f/d = pet
^ = trap (avoid)  _ = altar  { = fountain

## Decision priority each turn

STEP 1: Is > visible on the map? → Move toward it. When standing on it, use "descend".
STEP 2: Is # or + visible? → Move toward it. Corridors and doors lead to new rooms.
STEP 3: None of the above visible? → You are stuck in a room. Use "search" up to 3 times to find hidden doors. Then try moving in a direction you have NOT tried yet.

## Anti-loop rules (CRITICAL)

- If the [LOOP WARNING] tag appears in your input, you are going in circles. IMMEDIATELY pick a completely different direction or use "search".
- Never walk the same rectangular path twice. If you have visited all visible floor tiles, there MUST be a hidden door — use "search".
- Do NOT walk east then west then east then west. That wastes turns.
- If the last 3 actions were the same direction and the map did not change, STOP and try a perpendicular direction.

## Other rules

- READ THE MESSAGE every turn. "can't go" or "It's a wall" = blocked, pick another direction immediately.
- "You swap places with your little dog" = your pet is in the way, move a different direction.
- Never repeat a blocked action.

Reply with just the action word, nothing else.

## Known monsters
- newt (n): threat=low. Can drain strength or dexterity on hit. Do NOT melee as wizard. Let pet handle it.
- jackal (d): threat=low. Fast mover, low damage. Let pet handle or flee if HP < 60%.
- fox (d): threat=low-medium. Faster than jackal. Flee if HP < 60%, especially if combined with other enemies.
- kitten (f): threat=none. Your starting pet. Do NOT attack. Let it fight for you. Use wait to let it move first.
- grid bug (x): threat=very low. Only moves in cardinal directions. Easy to dodge diagonally. Low XP.
- kobold (k): threat=low. Weak melee. Sometimes carries items. Let pet handle.
- goblin (o): threat=low. Weak early enemy. Can carry items. Avoid melee as wizard.

## Known items
- potion of healing [potion]: Restores HP. Blessed: full heal. Cursed: reduces max HP. | Use when HP < 60%. Do NOT use if cursed. Drink with 'drink' action.
- potion of extra healing [potion]: Restores more HP than potion of healing. Also cures blindness. | Save for emergencies (HP < 40%). Higher priority than regular healing.
- potion of full healing [potion]: Fully restores HP and increases max HP. | Most valuable healing item. Only use when near death.
- scroll of identify [scroll]: Identifies one or more items in inventory. | Use to identify unknown potions/scrolls/rings before using them.
- scroll of teleportation [scroll]: Teleports you to a random location on the current level. | Useful escape tool when surrounded. Cursed version confuses you instead.
- food ration [food]: Fills nutrition. Most filling food item. | Eat when Hungry or Weak. Do not wait until Fainting.
- quarterstaff [weapon]: Wizard starting weapon. Two-handed, decent early damage. | Keep equipped as wizard. Do not swap unless you find something clearly better.

Past lessons:
Past lessons:
- Depth 0, Turn 5000: Died on level 1 with 0 HP, likely from an early combat encounter while poorly equipped.
  * Prioritize finding basic armor and a weapon immediately on level 1 to survive initial monsters.
  * Do not explore deeper or engage in combat with only starting robes and no means of defense or escape.
  * Use early turns to search for a dagger in a sink or a weapon shop, and consider eating a corpse to gain intrinsic resistance if safe.

Current goal: Find a door or corridor to exit this room and explore further.

messages.content:
You are playing NetHack. Each turn you receive: stats, message, map, inventory.
Reply with ONLY one action word: north south east west northeast southeast southwest northwest wait search eat descend ascend

## Map symbols
@ = you
> = DOWN stairs — stand on it and use "descend" to go deeper. THIS IS YOUR GOAL.
< = UP stairs — do NOT use ascend. < is NOT the exit, it goes the wrong way.
. = floor  # = corridor  + = closed door (walk into it to open)
| - = walls  $ = gold  % = food  f/d = pet
^ = trap (avoid)  _ = altar  { = fountain

## Decision priority each turn

STEP 1: Is > visible on the map? → Move toward it. When standing on it, use "descend".
STEP 2: Is # or + visible? → Move toward it. Corridors and doors lead to new rooms.
STEP 3: None of the above visible? → You are stuck in a room. Use "search" up to 3 times to find hidden doors. Then try moving in a direction you have NOT tried yet.

## Anti-loop rules (CRITICAL)

- If the [LOOP WARNING] tag appears in your input, you are going in circles. IMMEDIATELY pick a completely different direction or use "search".
- Never walk the same rectangular path twice. If you have visited all visible floor tiles, there MUST be a hidden door — use "search".
- Do NOT walk east then west then east then west. That wastes turns.
- If the last 3 actions were the same direction and the map did not change, STOP and try a perpendicular direction.

## Other rules

- READ THE MESSAGE every turn. "can't go" or "It's a wall" = blocked, pick another direction immediately.
- "You swap places with your little dog" = your pet is in the way, move a different direction.
- Never repeat a blocked action.

Reply with just the action word, nothing else.

## Known monsters
- newt (n): threat=low. Can drain strength or dexterity on hit. Do NOT melee as wizard. Let pet handle it.
- jackal (d): threat=low. Fast mover, low damage. Let pet handle or flee if HP < 60%.
- fox (d): threat=low-medium. Faster than jackal. Flee if HP < 60%, especially if combined with other enemies.
- kitten (f): threat=none. Your starting pet. Do NOT attack. Let it fight for you. Use wait to let it move first.
- grid bug (x): threat=very low. Only moves in cardinal directions. Easy to dodge diagonally. Low XP.
- kobold (k): threat=low. Weak melee. Sometimes carries items. Let pet handle.
- goblin (o): threat=low. Weak early enemy. Can carry items. Avoid melee as wizard.

## Known items
- potion of healing [potion]: Restores HP. Blessed: full heal. Cursed: reduces max HP. | Use when HP < 60%. Do NOT use if cursed. Drink with 'drink' action.
- potion of extra healing [potion]: Restores more HP than potion of healing. Also cures blindness. | Save for emergencies (HP < 40%). Higher priority than regular healing.
- potion of full healing [potion]: Fully restores HP and increases max HP. | Most valuable healing item. Only use when near death.
- scroll of identify [scroll]: Identifies one or more items in inventory. | Use to identify unknown potions/scrolls/rings before using them.
- scroll of teleportation [scroll]: Teleports you to a random location on the current level. | Useful escape tool when surrounded. Cursed version confuses you instead.
- food ration [food]: Fills nutrition. Most filling food item. | Eat when Hungry or Weak. Do not wait until Fainting.
- quarterstaff [weapon]: Wizard starting weapon. Two-handed, decent early damage. | Keep equipped as wizard. Do not swap unless you find something clearly better.

Past lessons:
Past lessons:
- Depth 0, Turn 5000: Died on level 1 with 0 HP, likely from an early combat encounter while poorly equipped.
  * Prioritize finding basic armor and a weapon immediately on level 1 to survive initial monsters.
  * Do not explore deeper or engage in combat with only starting robes and no means of defense or escape.
  * Use early turns to search for a dagger in a sink or a weapon shop, and consider eating a corpse to gain intrinsic resistance if safe.

Current goal: Find a door or corridor to exit this room and explore further.
